evidence-binding: r1-hook-assert-repair e1060655efe5

upstream_read: n5-hook-deletion bc92adfeddf3
upstream_read: n7-code-certify 4d6fb616a541

non_tdd_reason: five one-line narrowings of already-diagnosed stale assertions across four edition chains — no new behavior to write a failing unit test for; the failing oracle is the four edition chains, which must go green over the final tree.

regression-green: full sequential four-chain run (single invocation, `npm run test:kaola-workflow:claude && npm run test:kaola-workflow:codex && npm run test:kaola-workflow:gitlab && npm run test:kaola-workflow:gitea`, run in background, polled to completion, not looped or restarted) exited with `FOUR_CHAIN_EXIT=0`. All four `> kaola-workflow@6.23.1 test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` invocations ran in order and each reached its terminal "passed" line: claude -> "Workflow walkthrough simulation passed" + "test-generate-routing-surfaces: all 280 assertions passed."; codex -> "Kaola-Workflow Codex contract validation passed", "test409StableHomeSurvivesDirDeletion (#409): PASSED", "Kaola-Workflow walkthrough simulation passed"; gitlab -> "Kaola-Workflow GitLab contract validation passed", "GitLab workflow walkthrough simulation passed", "GitLab Codex workflow walkthrough simulation passed"; gitea -> "Kaola-Workflow Gitea contract validation passed", "Gitea workflow walkthrough simulation passed", "Gitea Codex workflow walkthrough simulation passed". Verified by grepping the full log for stack traces / uncaught throws (`at Object.<anonymous>`, `Node.js v25`, `throw err;`, `^Error:`) — zero hits. All `error`/`fail` substring hits in the log are pre-existing internal self-tests deliberately exercising failure paths (e.g. simulated GitHub TLS-timeout retries, run-chains.js's own "N chain(s) failed" self-test fixtures asserting its own reporting logic), not real chain failures — individually triaged, none touch any of the 7 files I edited.

## What was diagnosed (from n7-code-certify's five blocking findings, reproduced from n5-hook-deletion's hook deletion)

n5-hook-deletion deleted the two dormant advisory hooks (pre-commit, write-lane) across all four editions, narrowing all six hooks.json to exactly `SessionStart` (compact-context) + `SubagentStart` (subagent-dispatch-log). n7-code-certify's gate run (change_gate over the accumulated Phase C candidate) found five stale assertion/consumer surfaces this deletion tripped, none owned by any plan node's declared write set (R1-R4) or missed within an owned file (R5), all sharing the same fix shape: narrow the surface to the surviving two-hook set.

## The 5 diagnosed edits

1. `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/test-gitlab-workflow-scripts.js:2063` (R1) — the fresh-install hooks.json loop `for (const event of ['SessionStart', 'PreToolUse', 'SubagentStart'])` narrowed to `for (const event of ['SessionStart', 'SubagentStart'])`. Standalone reproduction before fix: `AssertionError [ERR_ASSERTION]: hooks.json must have entries for event PreToolUse` at :2065, exit 1. After fix: standalone run exits 0, "GitLab workflow script tests passed".
2. `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/test-gitea-workflow-scripts.js:2027` (R2) — `const requiredEvents = ['SessionStart', 'PreToolUse', 'SubagentStart'];` narrowed to `const requiredEvents = ['SessionStart', 'SubagentStart'];`. Standalone reproduction before fix: `AssertionError [ERR_ASSERTION]: hooks.json must have entries for PreToolUse` at :2030, exit 1. After fix: standalone run exits 0, "Gitea workflow script tests passed".
3. `scripts/test-validate-script-sync.js:142` (R3, section 6a drift RED-PROOF) — the fixture drift-plant moved from `rootHooks.hooks.PreToolUse.push({...})` to `rootHooks.hooks.SubagentStart.push({...})` (planted `id`, matcher, and the "test fixture only" comment left intact — only the target event key changed, since the real `hooks/hooks.json` no longer has a `PreToolUse` key and the expression was throwing `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'push')` at module load). Standalone reproduction after fix: `node scripts/test-validate-script-sync.js` exits 0, "validate-script-sync guard tests passed (51 assertions; 2 canonicalOnly exclusions machine-guarded)".
4. `scripts/sync-opencode-edition.js:73-77` (R4) — `HOOK_SCRIPTS` narrowed from `['kaola-workflow-pre-commit.sh', 'kaola-workflow-write-lane.sh', 'kaola-workflow-subagent-dispatch-log.sh']` to `['kaola-workflow-subagent-dispatch-log.sh']`, stopping `writeHooks`'s `readFileSync` of the two deleted canonical hook files (previously `ENOENT`) when the claude chain's `test-install-adaptive-config.js` shells `install-opencode.sh` to regenerate the `.opencode` tree. Standalone reproduction after fix: `node scripts/test-install-adaptive-config.js` exits 0, "Install adaptive-config tests passed". Did not otherwise touch opencode/kimi re-sync, `opencode.json`, the plugin adapter, or `test-opencode-edition.js` (Phase D scope, per instruction).
5. `plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/simulate-kaola-workflow-walkthrough.js:736` (R5, `test409StableHomeSurvivesDirDeletion`) — `assert(commandCount >= 4, '#409: expected the four managed hook commands, saw ' + commandCount)` narrowed to `assert(commandCount >= 2, '#409: expected the two managed hook commands, saw ' + commandCount)`. Canonical `scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` (which carries no #409/PreToolUse assertion) was correctly left untouched. Standalone reproduction after fix: `node plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/simulate-kaola-workflow-walkthrough.js` exits 0, "Kaola-Workflow walkthrough simulation passed"; confirmed again inside the full codex chain run ("test409StableHomeSurvivesDirDeletion (#409): PASSED").

## Extra same-class fixes found and applied (not in the original n7 diagnosis, but inside the declared write set)

Standalone reproduction of the R1/R2-fixed files (steps 1-2 above) surfaced a further stale hook-count assertion in each file's own `test409StableHomeSurvivesDirDeletion`, identical in shape to R5 but in a different file:

- `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/test-gitlab-workflow-scripts.js:2005` — `assert.ok(commandCount >= 4, '#409 gl: expected the four managed hook commands, saw ' + commandCount);` narrowed to `assert.ok(commandCount >= 2, '#409 gl: expected the two managed hook commands, saw ' + commandCount);`.
- `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/test-gitea-workflow-scripts.js:1971` — `assert.ok(commandCount >= 4, '#409 gt: expected the four managed hook commands, saw ' + commandCount);` narrowed to `assert.ok(commandCount >= 2, '#409 gt: expected the two managed hook commands, saw ' + commandCount);`.

Both surfaces reproduced red standalone before the fix (`AssertionError: #409 gl/gt: expected the four managed hook commands, saw 2`) and green after (full "GitLab/Gitea workflow script tests passed"). Both files are already inside my declared write set (`plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/test-gitlab-workflow-scripts.js`, `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/test-gitea-workflow-scripts.js`), so this is treated as an in-scope mechanical repair of the same root-cause class already established by R5, not a new write-set gap requiring replan routing.

## Verification sequence

1. Read seeded evidence file (header only) + upstream n5-hook-deletion.md and n7-code-certify.md (found under `kaola-workflow/issue-725/.cache/epochs/1/files/.cache/`, not the live `.cache/` — the live directory only holds this epoch's own artifacts).
2. Applied the 5 diagnosed edits.
3. Standalone-reproduced each of the 5 previously-red surfaces individually — all 5 green, PLUS discovered and fixed the 2 extra same-class needles above (also standalone-reproduced red-to-green).
4. Ran the full four-chain sequentially exactly once in the background (`npm run test:kaola-workflow:claude && ...codex && ...gitlab && ...gitea`), polled to completion without restarting or looping. Exit `FOUR_CHAIN_EXIT=0`. Confirmed via full-log grep for uncaught stack traces (zero hits) and confirmed each chain's terminal "passed" lines are present in order.

## Write-set gaps

None. All fixes (the 5 diagnosed + the 2 same-class extras) landed inside the declared write set: `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/test-gitlab-workflow-scripts.js`, `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/test-gitea-workflow-scripts.js`, `scripts/test-validate-script-sync.js`, `scripts/sync-opencode-edition.js`, `plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/simulate-kaola-workflow-walkthrough.js`. No `PreToolUse` entry was re-added anywhere; the surviving `SessionStart`/compact-context and `SubagentStart`/subagent-dispatch-log hooks and their assertions were left untouched throughout.
